comment: K₄ agent worker tetrahedron — four hub vertices the personal tetrahedron docks to. v1.1.0 adds: signed Ed25519 dock + per-call envelope, K₄,₄,₄ triadic cover with the family cage (will/sj/wj/christyn), federation peer registry, and a real skill dispatcher (local Ollama HTTP with structured-echo fallback). Pairs with k4-personal/personal-tetra.js (structure/connection/rhythm/creation) and the k4-cage/family vertices. Source of truth for agents.html, packages/k4-agent-hub/, verify:k4-agent-hub, and any future hub-product card.

topology.shapeStack: K₄,₄,₄ — three layers (family, personal, agent), four edges each, twelve cross-stack edges total

appearance.comment: Four K₄ anchors (teal/coral/phosphorus/lavender) used in agents.html and any future hub card. Butter is reserved for the operator's central pillar (not a vertex; the inscribed pyramid apex).

vertices.name: FORGE
vertices.title: Make — code, deploy, scaffold
vertices.subtitle: Where personal structure meets the anvil.

vertices.name: COUNSEL
vertices.title: Protect — legal, triage, after-incident
vertices.subtitle: Where personal connection meets the shield.

vertices.name: SCHOLAR
vertices.title: Understand — research, grants, pattern-finding
vertices.subtitle: Where personal rhythm meets the long view.

vertices.name: SCRIBE
vertices.title: Remember — passport, accommodation log, foundry
vertices.subtitle: Where personal creation meets the archive.

bipartiteCover.comment: Each personal-tetrahedron vertex docks to one agent hub. Together they form K₄,₄ — eight nodes, sixteen edges (six personal + six agent + four bipartite docks).

triadicCover.comment: Family-cage K₄ adds a third layer below the personal tetrahedron. Each family vertex pairs with a personal dock and (transitively) with an agent hub. Children's vertices route to SCRIBE through the child-mesh-unlock gate.

edgeBreakdown.note: Agent K₄ + Personal K₄ + Family K₄ = 18 graph edges; the K₄,₄,₄ cover layer adds 12 cross-stack edges (4+4+4). Total connection map = 30.

endpoints.purpose: Personal tetrahedron requests a session at one or all hubs.

request.clientId: string (UUID; the personal tetrahedron's stable identity)
request.capabilities: array of skill ids the client wants
request.publicKey: optional Ed25519 public key (future: signed dock)

response.policies: rate limit, max body size, child-gate hints

endpoints.purpose: Invoke a skill on the named hub. {hub} ∈ forge|counsel|scholar|scribe.
endpoints.response: object (per-skill output) or text/event-stream if stream=true

endpoints.purpose: Returns the K₄ adjacency, hub statuses, and the bipartite cover with the calling personal tetrahedron.
Inter-hub edge call (e.g., FORGE asks SCHOLAR to brief). Routes through the K₄ adjacency map; may aggregate.

auth.rotation: refresh after 23h or on policy change

signedDock.envelope: { body, ts, nonce, sig } where sig = Ed25519(clientId | personalTetra.schema | sortedCaps | ts | nonce)

signedCallEnvelope.replayProtection: nonce stored in KV with 90s TTL

websocket.purpose: Optional duplex stream for long-running skill invocations (build/research/triage).

skillDispatcher.comment: Each hub's runSkill(skill, input) tries the local Ollama HTTP API first when env OLLAMA_BASE_URL is set, otherwise returns a structured echo. Wiring to simplex-v7 cloud lanes is queued under CWP-P31-K4-AGENT-HUB-WIRE-SIMPLEX-LANE for v1.2.0.

ollama.fallback: structured echo with skill metadata + dispatcherUsed='echo'

crossEdge.comment: Inter-hub edge calls use Cloudflare Durable Object service-binding stubs (not HTTP). The Worker entry routes /v1/cross/{from}/{to} to env[HUB_BINDINGS[to]].get(...).fetch() — sub-millisecond, no public network hop.

federation.comment: Multiple operator instances of the K₄ agent tetrahedron can register as peers. Each peer presents its dock manifest URL and a signed instanceId; /v1/federation aggregates topologies. v1.1.0 ships the registry and aggregator; peer-to-peer signed messaging lands in v1.2.0.

endpoints.purpose: Register a peer hub. Body: { instanceId, manifestUrl, publicKey, ts, sig }.
Aggregated topology — local + cached peer manifests.

metrics.format: JSON (per-hub counters: calls_total, errors_total, avg_ms, last_call_at, dispatcher_used)
metrics.comment: Light footprint — pulled from per-DO calls table; aggregated by the Worker entry.

deployment.preferred: single Cloudflare Worker with four Durable Object classes (one per hub) + KV for cross-hub session pinning

live.comment: Both URLs are placeholders until wrangler deploy runs with a CF token. Operator may switch to a custom domain once registered.

ethicsAndGuards.noChildExposureWithoutGate: SCRIBE.phos-companion is gated on child-mesh-unlock (mirror of cognitive-passport audience-matrix row 8).
ethicsAndGuards.noOperatorImpersonation: FORGE / COUNSEL / SCHOLAR / SCRIBE are agents; they do not represent themselves as the operator.
ethicsAndGuards.noCredentialPassThrough: Hubs never pass through user credentials to model providers. The local Ollama lane is preferred for confidential work (per .cursor/rules/p31-ollama-fleet.mdc).
ethicsAndGuards.allHubsRespectSpoonState: If session.executiveSpoonState is 'low' or 'crisis', hubs reduce branching and prefer one-step replies.

publicMessaging.tagline: Four hubs. One mesh. Your tetrahedron, their anvil-shield-book-quill.
publicMessaging.elevator: P31's K₄ agent tetrahedron is four cooperating workers — Forge, Counsel, Scholar, Scribe — that your personal tetrahedron docks to. Each hub speaks one verb (make, protect, understand, remember). The six K₄ edges let them brief each other so a single ask reaches the right pair without the operator having to route it.
